pr.title: [WIP] [WIP] Address feedback on logging silent failures in uninstall.php switch_to_blog
pr.body:
Thanks for the feedback on #7. I've created this new PR, which merges into #7, to address your comment. I will work on the changes and keep this PR's description up to date as I make progress.

Original PR: #7
Triggering review: https://github.com/Starisian-Technologies/sparxstar-3iatlas-rlc-ui/pull/7#pullrequestreview-4351483331
> @copilot - VIOLATIONS (must fix before merge)
> [HIGH] BootstrapRuntime.php — guard changed from WPINC to ABSPATH without documented rationale
> php// OLD (removed)
> if (!defined('WPINC')) { die; }
> 
> // NEW
> if ( ! defined( 'ABSPATH' ) ) { exit; }
> WPINC is the correct WordPress direct-access guard — it's defined earlier in the WP boot sequence and is the conventional guard for plugin files. ABSPATH is defined at wp-load.php and is equally valid, but changing this silently without documentation is a drift risk. The platform standard is WPINC for plugin files; ABSPATH is the correct guard for uninstall.php (which this PR correctly uses). The change here inverts the convention. → Revert BootstrapRuntime.php guard to WPINC, keep ABSPATH only in uninstall.php where it is correct.
> 
> [HIGH] uninstall.php — switch_to_blog() return value check is insufficient
> phpif ( switch_to_blog( (int) $site_id ) ) {
>     spx_aiwa_delete_site_options();
>     restore_current_blog();
> }
> switch_to_blog() returns true on success and false when the site doesn't exist or is archived. If it returns false, the code silently skips that site — no error, no log, no dead-letter. Per platform standard: No Silent Failure. Every failure must log internally with full context. → Add an else branch that logs the failure with $site_id before continuing.
> 
> [MEDIUM] AiwaSessionManager.php — is_teacher flag computed from get_current_user_id() at join time, not verified at session creation
> php$current_user_id = get_current_user_id();
> $teacher_id = (int) get_post_meta($session_id, 'teacher_id', true);
> $is_teacher = $current_user_id > 0 && $current_user_id === $teacher_id;
> This is correct logic for the join flow but introduces a trust question: a student who joins before the teacher meta is set, or in a race condition window, could have is_teacher = false even if they are the teacher. More critically: this is_teacher flag stored in post meta becomes the single authority used in find_teacher_participant() for the leaderboard ceremony — which awards the teacher_award. The flag is stored on join; it is never re-verified at ceremony time. The teacher_award must be authoritative, not derived from a stored flag set at an unverified moment. → Flag for human review — architectural question about teacher authority verification. The fix likely requires: at ceremony time, re-verify teacher identity against teacher_id post meta + WP user ID, not just the stored is_teacher flag.
> 
> WARNINGS (should fix)
> [MEDIUM] AiwaRestApi.php — ceremony fallback creates a synthetic participant entry directly in the $participants array
> php$participants[ $teacher_participant_id ] = array(
>     'display_name' => $teacher_display_name,
>     'joined_at'    => (int) get_post_meta( $session_id, 'started_at', true ),
>     'wp_user_id'   => $teacher_id,
>     'is_teacher'   => true,
> );
> This mutates the $participants array in-memory for response purposes only. It does NOT persist to post meta. That is the correct behavior. However it is not documented as such — a future developer reading this will not know if this is intentional or a missing update_post_meta() call. → Add an inline comment: // Ceremony-only: not persisted. Teacher is a display-only entry for this response.
> [MEDIUM] AiwaTrigramIndex.php — maybe_create_table() replaces dbDelta()
> php$created = maybe_create_table( $table, $sql );
> maybe_create_table() only creates the table if it doesn't exist — it does not update the schema when the table already exists and the schema has changed. dbDelta() handles both creation and schema migration. If the trigram table schema ever needs to change (adding a column, changing an index), maybe_create_table() will silently do nothing on existing installs. The PR description says "Safe to call multiple times — maybe_create_table() is idempotent" — that's only true for the create case, not for schema migration. → Document this limitation explicitly in a comment. If schema evolution is anticipated, revert to dbDelta(). This is an architectural choice that Max needs to make consciously.
> [LOW] BootstrapDefinitions.php — spx_aiwa_get_network_site_ids() is a global function, not a class method
> It's well-written and correctly paginated. But it adds a global function to the WordPress namespace rather than being a static method on Sparxstar3iAtlasRlc or a private utility class. Platform standard is to minimize global function footprint. → Consider moving to a static method in the main plugin class or a dedicated NetworkUtils class.
> [LOW] ai_manifest.json — spx_aiwa_delete_site_options added but spx_aiwa_get_network_site_ids listed before it
> Both are correctly registered. No functional issue. Ordering in the manifest should follow load order or file order for readability. Minor housekeeping.
> 
> FLAG FOR HUMAN REVIEW
> Teacher authority model — architectural
> The teacher_award is now issued via a two-path fallback: (1) find teacher in participants by is_teacher flag or wp_user_id match, (2) if not found, synthesize a wp-teacher-{id} entry from WP user data. Path (2) means a teacher who never joined the session still receives the teacher_award in the ceremony response.
> Question for Max: Is that intentional? Should the teacher_award require that the teacher actually participated in the session, or is it always issued regardless of teacher presence? This is a pedagogical/gamification decision, not a code decision. The code correctly implements "always award the teacher" — but that may or may not be the right rule.
> → Consider: sparxstar-architect if this connects to the broader points/myCred governance model.
> 
> MECHANICAL — Run Before Merge
> 
> Run PHPCS / super-linter — spacing and bracket style changed throughout (tabs vs. spaces, brace placement); verify PHPCS WordPressCS baseline is enforced uniformly.
> Run PHPStan level 5 — new return type void on create_table() added; verify no downstream callers check the return value.
> Run sparxstar-standards-validate — confirm no MIT license drift in composer.json.
> 
> 
> VERDICT: CONDITIONAL
> 
> Two HIGH issues must resolve before merge:
> 1. Revert BootstrapRuntime.php guard to `WPINC`
> 2. Add silent-failure logging to uninstall.php switch_to_blog() branch
> 
> One architectural question for Max:
> → Does teacher_award require teacher presence in session, or is it always issued?
> 
> All MEDIUM/LOW items are non-blocking but should be addressed in this sprint.
> 
> maybe_create_table() is a WordPress ecosystem directive, not a Copilot drift. That clears the dbDelta() warning.
> Updating the verdict accordingly:
> REVISED VERDICT: CONDITIONAL
> 
> HIGH (must fix before merge):
> 1. Revert BootstrapRuntime.php guard back to `WPINC`
> 2. Add logging to uninstall.php switch_to_blog() failure branch
> 
> CLEARED:
> - maybe_create_table() — WordPress directive, accepted
> 
> Architectural question still open:
> → Does teacher_award require teacher presence in session, or always issued?
> Now — the WPINC vs ABSPATH guard change. ABSPATH + exit is the WordPress Core standard. The HIGH violation on BootstrapRuntime.php is withdrawn.
> REVISED VERDICT: CONDITIONAL — one blocker remaining
> 
> CLEARED:
> - BootstrapRuntime.php ABSPATH guard — WordPress Core standard, confirmed
> - maybe_create_table() — WordPress directive, confirmed
> 
> HIGH (one remaining blocker):
> - uninstall.php switch_to_blog() silent failure — no log on false return
> 
> Locked. Now writing the Copilot fix instructions for PR #15.
> 
> Copilot Fix Instructions — sparxstar-3iatlas-rlc PR #15
> Issue title: fix(bootstrap): log silent failure in uninstall network traversal
> File: uninstall.php
> What to fix and why:
> switch_to_blog() returns false when a site ID is invalid, archived, or deleted between when get_sites() ran and when the loop executes. The current code silently skips those sites — no log, no record. Platform standard: no silent failure. Every failure must log internally with full context.
> Exact change required:
> php// CURRENT
> foreach ( $sites as $site_id ) {
>     if ( switch_to_blog( (int) $site_id ) ) {
>         spx_aiwa_delete_site_options();
>         restore_current_blog();
>     }
> }
> 
> // REQUIRED
> foreach ( $sites as $site_id ) {
>     if ( switch_to_blog( (int) $site_id ) ) {
>         spx_aiwa_delete_site_options();
>         restore_current_blog();
>     } else {
>         // Site unavailable during uninstall traversal — log and continue.
>         // Platform standard: no silent failure. Full context required.
>         error_log(
>             sprintf(
>                 '[sparxstar-3iatlas-rlc] uninstall: switch_to_blog() failed for site_id=%d. Site may be archived, deleted, or unavailable.',
>                 (int) $site_id
>             )
>         );
>     }
> }
> Do not change anything else in this file.
> After this fix, PR #15 is clear to merge.
pr.author: Copilot
